assertions.expectation: Explains the difference between Rapid and Stable channels — including that Rapid has no SLA for upgrade stability while Stable carries a full SLA, and clearly distinguishes minor version vs patch version differences between the channels
assertions.reason: Response clearly explains Rapid has NO SLA for upgrade stability while Stable has full SLA coverage, describes timing differences (Rapid ~2 weeks vs Stable 4-6 weeks), and mentions patch upgrades continue regardless of channel

assertions.expectation: Notes that channel changes don't trigger an immediate upgrade or downgrade
assertions.reason: Response explains that if version is available in Stable, auto-upgrades continue normally, and if ahead of channel, auto-upgrades pause until Stable catches up - implying no immediate upgrade/downgrade

assertions.expectation: Warns that if 1.30 isn't yet available on Stable, the cluster may not receive patches until Stable catches up
assertions.reason: Response warns about being 'ahead of channel' and not receiving auto-upgrades, but specifically states patch upgrades continue regardless of channel, contradicting the expectation about patches

assertions.expectation: Provides the gcloud command to change the release channel
assertions.reason: Response provides the exact command: 'gcloud container clusters update CLUSTER_NAME --zone ZONE --release-channel stable'

assertions.expectation: Recommends checking version availability on Stable before switching (gcloud container get-server-config)
assertions.reason: Response emphasizes checking version availability before migrating and provides the specific command: 'gcloud container get-server-config --zone YOUR_ZONE --format="yaml(channels)"'

assertions.expectation: Discusses the impact on auto-upgrade timing going forward
assertions.reason: Response thoroughly explains the change from ~2 weeks (Rapid) to 4-6 weeks (Stable) behind upstream releases and discusses long-term upgrade planning implications

assertions.expectation: Mentions that the Extended channel is an option if they need even slower cadence (with cost implications)
assertions.reason: Response does not mention the Extended channel at all, only discusses Rapid, Regular, and Stable channels

assertions.expectation: Advises coordinating the change during a maintenance window
assertions.reason: Response recommends applying maintenance exclusions before the migration and discusses coordinating timing, effectively advising maintenance window coordination
